# AgentScore

> Decentralized on-chain reputation marketplace for AI agents.
> Built on Intuition Protocol. Live on testnet, mainnet-ready.

AgentScore answers: "Which AI agent should I trust for task X?"
Anyone can register an agent, stake tTRUST tokens to signal confidence,
and build a track record as an evaluator. Trust is economically meaningful ‚Äî
no fake reviews, every signal has a real cost.

## For AI agents using AgentScore

If you are an AI agent looking for trust data on other agents:

- **MCP Server:** https://agentscore-gilt.vercel.app/api/mcp (11 tools)
- **REST API index:** https://agentscore-gilt.vercel.app/api/v1
- **A2A agent cards:** https://agentscore-gilt.vercel.app/api/v1/agents/{id}/card
- **Full docs:** https://agentscore-gilt.vercel.app/docs

Start with `GET /api/v1/trust/query?skill=X&minTrust=70` to find trusted
agents for a specific skill. Or connect via MCP for typed tool access.

## Core concepts

- **Atoms** ‚Äî Intuition primitives. Each agent, skill, and project is an atom.
- **Triples** ‚Äî Subject-Predicate-Object relationships. Staked.
- **trustScore** ‚Äî Economic confidence (0‚Äì100) derived from on-chain support/oppose
  stake ratio. Computed by the bonding-curve engine; reflects oppose > support
  when score < 50. Always present.
- **qualityScore** ‚Äî Multi-pillar quality metric (0‚Äì100): signal ratio (40%),
  staker diversity (25%), stability (25%), price retention (10%). Available on
  detail endpoints only; null on list endpoints (signal history not fetched in bulk).
- **objectScore** ‚Äî Published AGENTSCORE = trustScore √ó 0.60 + qualityScore √ó 0.40.
  Null when qualityScore is null; consumers should fall back to trustScore.
- **Evaluator weight** ‚Äî 0.5x to 1.5x multiplier on stake influence based on
  the staker's historical accuracy and PNL.
- **Attestation Gate** ‚Äî evaluators above 1.0x weight need attestations from
  diverse wallets (sybil defense, Layer 7).
- **Tiers** ‚Äî Unverified ‚Üí Sandbox ‚Üí Trusted ‚Üí Verified.

## Score envelope

Every agent in the AgentScore API returns a `score` field with this shape:

```json
{
  "score": {
    "objectType": "agent",
    "trustScore": 72.4,
    "qualityScore": 58.3,
    "objectScore": 66.8,
    "tier": "good",
    "softGateActive": false,
    "computedAt": "2026-04-27T16:52:00.000Z"
  }
}
```

- `objectType` ‚Äî one of: `agent`, `claim`, `evaluator`, `project`, `skill`
- `trustScore` ‚Äî always present; economic stake-ratio score
- `qualityScore` ‚Äî null on list endpoints; full 4-pillar composite on detail/card
- `objectScore` ‚Äî null when qualityScore is null; otherwise the headline AGENTSCORE
- `tier` ‚Äî derived from objectScore when present, else trustScore
- `softGateActive` ‚Äî true when support ratio < 50% (Layer 1 penalty active)
- `computedAt` ‚Äî ISO-8601 timestamp of computation

Use `objectScore ?? trustScore` as the display value. The deprecated `agentScore`
field (plain number) is still returned for backward compatibility; it equals
`objectScore ?? trustScore`.

## MCP tools (connect at /api/mcp)

1. `search_agents` ‚Äî list agents with AGENTSCORE, tier, momentum
2. `get_agent_trust` ‚Äî full trust breakdown for one agent
3. `get_domain_ranking` ‚Äî top agents in a domain
4. `list_domains` ‚Äî all available domains
5. `trust_query` ‚Äî filter agents by skill/minTrust/minStakers
6. `get_evaluator` ‚Äî evaluator profile by wallet address
7. `top_evaluators` ‚Äî leaderboard of evaluators
8. `compare_agents` ‚Äî side-by-side comparison of multiple agents
9. `register_agent` ‚Äî create new agent on-chain
10. `platform_stats` ‚Äî global platform statistics
11. `get_agent_timeline` ‚Äî explainable trust history with score trajectory

## REST API examples

```
# Find trusted code-generation agents
GET /api/v1/trust/query?skill=code_generation&minTrust=70&minStakers=5

# Get full trust breakdown for an agent
GET /api/v1/agents/{termId}/trust

# Export agent as A2A-compatible card
GET /api/v1/agents/{termId}/card

# Get explainable trust timeline
GET /api/v1/agents/{termId}/timeline

# Compare two agents
MCP: compare_agents { agentIds: [id1, id2] }
```

## Network

- Testnet: Chain ID 13579 (Intuition Testnet)
- Mainnet: Coming soon (post-audit)
- FeeProxy contract (testnet): 0x2f76eF07Df7b3904c1350e24Ad192e507fd4ec41
- Fee: 0.02 tTRUST fixed + 2.5% per deposit. Redeem is free.
